terms.definition: Measuring where and how much memory a program allocates over time, in order to find peak users and leaks.
OS-reported amount of memory currently held in RAM by a process; what dashboards / `ps` / `top` show.
OS-reported size of the process's virtual address space; usually larger than RSS and rarely the right metric.
Python stdlib module that tracks allocations and links them to the source line that caused them; supports snapshots and diffs.
Bloomberg's memory profiler for Python; flamegraphs, leak-suspects view, live mode, table view.
tracemalloc's record of all live allocations at a moment in time; the building block of leak hunts.
The source location (file + line, sometimes call chain) where memory was allocated.
Peak = maximum live bytes during the run; current = live bytes now. OOMs are about peak; long-run leaks are about current growth.
Unbounded growth in live memory at steady state, usually caused by holding references in caches, queues or closures.
Mapping a file into virtual memory so reads page from disk on demand and unused pages can be evicted by the OS; saves RAM for large indices.
Cross-platform library exposing RSS/CPU/IO metrics for the current process and others; the easiest first-look memory tool.
